In [1]:
%matplotlib inline

Marginal Fitting: Is Employed

  • is_employed is fixed to match the proportions per age predicted by the BFS
  • The choice of persons to fix is done with a probability model based on their current_edu and current_job_rank
In [2]:

import sys

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

%%time
synpop_persons = Persons(YEAR)
synpop_persons.load_pickle(SYNPOP_PERSONS_FILE)

fixed_edu = pd.read_pickle(os.path.join(FIXED_ATTRIBUTES_DIR, f'fixed_current_edu_SMART.pickle.gzip'), compression='gzip')
synpop_persons.overwrite_column('current_edu', fixed_edu)

fixed_job = pd.read_pickle(os.path.join(FIXED_ATTRIBUTES_DIR, f'fixed_current_job_rank_SMART.pickle.gzip'), compression='gzip')
synpop_persons.overwrite_column('current_job_rank', fixed_job)

persons = synpop_persons.data[COLUMNS_OF_INTEREST].copy(deep=True)

del synpop_persons

By Age¶

In [17]:
# This is a function that is only used for this Notebook so stays here
def prepare_bfs_level_of_activity_marginal_table(bfs_avg_active_people_by_age, all_ages_set, year):
    
    prop_active = (bfs_avg_active_people_by_age.reindex(all_ages_set, fill_value=0) / 100).to_frame()
    prop_inactive = 1 - prop_active

    prop_active['is_employed'] = True
    prop_active = prop_active.reset_index().set_index(['age', 'is_employed'])
    prop_inactive['is_employed'] = False
    prop_inactive = prop_inactive.reset_index().set_index(['age', 'is_employed'])

    bfs_prop_predictions = pd.concat([prop_active, prop_inactive]).sort_index()

    bfs_prop_predictions = bfs_prop_predictions.iloc[:, 0].rename(f'expected_ratios_{year}')
    return bfs_prop_predictions

Scenario Year

In [18]:
active_pop_client = FSO_ActivePopPredictionsClient(granularity='age').load(YEAR)
active_pop_stats_by_age = active_pop_client.stats

all_ages = set(persons['age'].unique())
bfs_predicted_ratio = prepare_bfs_level_of_activity_marginal_table(active_pop_stats_by_age['avg_active_people'], all_ages, YEAR)

FSO without job seekers¶

  • BFS predicts active people which includes job seekers.
  • In MOBi, all working people go to a job, job seekers have level_of_employment = 0.
  • Based on the BFS own definition, the unemployement rate in 2017 is about 4.5%.

References:

  • https://www.seco.admin.ch/seco/fr/home/wirtschaftslage---wirtschaftspolitik/Wirtschaftslage/Arbeitslosenzahlen.html
  • https://www.bfs.admin.ch/bfs/fr/home/statistiques/travail-remuneration/activite-professionnelle-temps-travail/personnes-actives/scenarios-population-active.assetdetail.9687328.html
In [20]:
# The very conservative assuption of 2% unemployement has been made for 2040.
UNEMPLOYMENT_RATE = 0.02

bfs_without_unemployed = bfs_predicted_ratio.copy(deep=True)
bfs_without_unemployed.loc[:, True] = bfs_without_unemployed.loc[:, True].values * (1 - UNEMPLOYMENT_RATE)
bfs_without_unemployed.loc[:, False] = (1 - bfs_without_unemployed.loc[:, True]).values

synpop_employed = persons.groupby('is_employed').count().iloc[:, 0].loc[True]
bfs_active_abs = active_pop_stats_global['active_people'].iloc[0]
bfs_active_rel = active_pop_stats_global['avg_active_people'].iloc[0]

global_stats = pd.DataFrame({'pop': {'synpop-raw': persons.shape[0], 
                                     'bfs': int(bfs_active_abs / bfs_active_rel * 100)},
                             'active': {'synpop-raw': synpop_employed, 
                                        'bfs': bfs_active_abs},
                             '% active': {'synpop-raw': (synpop_employed / persons.shape[0] * 100).round(1), 
                                          'bfs': bfs_active_rel.round(1)}
                            }
                           )
print(YEAR)
print('BFS numbers printed here STILL contain unemployed people')
global_stats

global_stats_ist = pd.DataFrame({'pop': {'synpop-raw': persons_ist.shape[0], 
                                     'bfs': int(bfs_active_abs_ist / bfs_active_rel_ist * 100)},
                                 'active': {'synpop-raw': synpop_employed_ist, 
                                            'bfs': bfs_active_abs_ist},
                                 '% active': {'synpop-raw': (synpop_employed_ist / persons_ist.shape[0] * 100).round(1),

Marginals per Age¶

Scenario Year

In [26]:
%%time
# marginals keeping in unemployment 
marginals_1 = marginal_fitting.compute_bfs_vs_scenario_marginal_summary_table(persons, 
                                                                              bfs_predicted_ratio, 
                                                                              YEAR, 
                                                                              feature='is_employed', 
                                                                              control_level_list=['age']
                                                                             )

Wall time: 10.1 s
In [27]:
counts_per_cat = (marginals_1
                  .reset_index()
                  .astype({'is_employed': str})  # to avoid boolean values as column names
                  .pivot(index='age', columns='is_employed', values=f'counts_{YEAR}')
                  .rename(columns={'True': 'true', 'False': 'false'})  # to avoid confusion between bool and str
                  .fillna(0).astype(int)
                 )
counts_per_cat = counts_per_cat[['true', 'false']]
In [28]:
expected_true_counts = (marginals_1[f'expected_counts_{YEAR}']
                         .reset_index()
                         .query('is_employed')
                         .astype({'is_employed': str})  # to avoid boolean values as column names
                         .groupby('age').sum().iloc[:, 0]
                         .rename('bfs: is_employed prediction')
                    )
expected_true_counts.index = list(expected_true_counts.index)  # otherwise, categorical
In [29]:
title = f'Employed People by Age - SynPop{YEAR}-raw'

ax = plot_binary_feature_per_age_with_marginals(counts_per_cat,
                                                expected_true_counts, 
                                                colour_dict=COLOUR_DICT, 
                                                ymax=150_000, 
                                                y_grid=True, 
                                                title=title
                                               )
    
save_figure(SAVE_OUTPUTS, f'05a_SynPop{YEAR}_raw_is_employed_by_age.{FIG_FORMAT}',
            OUTPUT_DIR, FIG_DPI, FIG_FORMAT, FIG_BBOX_INCHES, FIG_PAD_INCHES)

SMART Fixing methodology¶

Expected Counts¶

In [40]:
%%time
# marginals without unemployment 
marginals_2 = marginal_fitting.compute_bfs_vs_scenario_marginal_summary_table(persons, 
                                                                              bfs_without_unemployed, 
                                                                              YEAR, 
                                                                              feature='is_employed', 
                                                                              control_level_list=['age']
                                                                             )

Wall time: 10.1 s
In [41]:
expected_pop_counts = (marginals_2
                       .reset_index()
                       .astype({'is_employed': str})  # to avoid boolean values as column names
                       .pivot(index='age', columns='is_employed', values=f'expected_counts_{YEAR}')
                       .rename(columns={'True': 'true', 'False': 'false'})  # to avoid confusion between bool and str
                       .fillna(0).astype(int)
                       )
expected_pop_counts = expected_pop_counts[['true', 'false']]

Probability Model¶

In [42]:
ct_empl_ist = marginal_fitting.compute_cross_table(persons_ist,
                                                   main_feature='is_employed',
                                                   secondary_features=['current_edu', 'current_job_rank']
                                                   )
In [43]:
ct_empl_ist.head()
Out[43]:
is_employed False True
current_edu current_job_rank
kindergarten apprentice 0 0
employee 0 0
management 0 0
null 0 0
pupil_primary apprentice 0 0
In [44]:
%%time
person_proba = marginal_fitting.build_person_proba_with_from_cross_table(
    persons, ct_empl_ist, 
    merge_on=['current_edu', 'current_job_rank']
)

Wall time: 5.93 s

Fixing...¶

The logging can be set to INFO or DEBUG (at the top of the notebook logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)) based on how much detail is needed!

In [45]:
logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
In [46]:
fixed_empl = marginal_fitting.fix_categorical_feature(persons=persons,
                                                      feature='is_employed',
                                                      pop_segment_variables=['age'],
                                                      control_totals=expected_pop_counts,
                                                      person_proba=person_proba
                                                      )

fixed_empl = fixed_empl == 'true'  # Turning it back into a boolean

2020-04-22 17:11:11,969 - INFO - synpop.marginal_fitting: Fixing "is_employed" by population segments based on: ['age']

2020-04-22 17:11:26,085 - INFO - synpop.marginal_fitting: 40 / 132 population segments fixed...

2020-04-22 17:11:32,391 - INFO - synpop.marginal_fitting: All 132 population segments fixed!
In [47]:
persons_fixed = persons.copy(deep=True)
persons_fixed = persons_fixed.drop('is_employed', axis=1)
persons_fixed = pd.merge(persons_fixed, fixed_empl, left_on='person_id', right_index=True)
persons_fixed.shape
Out[47]:
(10037464, 16)

Marginals per Age¶

Fixed SynPop

In [50]:
%%time
marginals_fixed = marginal_fitting.compute_bfs_vs_scenario_marginal_summary_table(persons_fixed, 
                                                                                  bfs_predicted_ratio, 
                                                                                  YEAR, 
                                                                                  feature='is_employed', 
                                                                                  control_level_list=['age']
                                                                                 )

counts_per_cat_fixed = (marginals_fixed
                       .reset_index()
                       .astype({'is_employed': str})  # to avoid boolean values as column names
                       .pivot(index='age', columns='is_employed', values=f'counts_{YEAR}')
                       .rename(columns={'True': 'true', 'False': 'false'})  # to avoid confusion between bool and str
                       )
counts_per_cat_fixed = counts_per_cat_fixed[['true', 'false']]

expected_true_counts_fixed =  (marginals_fixed[f'expected_counts_{YEAR}']
                               .reset_index()
                               .query('is_employed')
                               .astype({'is_employed': str})  # to avoid boolean values as column names
                               .groupby('age').sum().iloc[:, 0]
                               .rename('bfs: is_employed prediction')
                               )
expected_true_counts_fixed.index = list(expected_true_counts_fixed.index)  # otherwise, categorical

Wall time: 9.59 s
In [51]:
title = f'Employed People by Age - SynPop{YEAR}+ (before consistency rules)'

ax = plot_binary_feature_per_age_with_marginals(counts_per_cat_fixed,
                                                expected_true_counts, 
                                                colour_dict=COLOUR_DICT, 
                                                ymax=150_000, 
                                                y_grid=True, 
                                                title=title
                                               )
    
save_figure(SAVE_OUTPUTS, f'05a_SynPop{YEAR}Plus_is_employed_by_age_no_consistency.{FIG_FORMAT}',
            OUTPUT_DIR, FIG_DPI, FIG_FORMAT, FIG_BBOX_INCHES, FIG_PAD_INCHES)

2020-04-22 17:12:17,925 - INFO - root: Figure saved to file : ./outputs\05a_SynPop2040Plus_is_employed_by_age_no_consistency.png

Cross-Relationships¶

Fixed SynPop

CT1

In [52]:
ax, ct1_fixed = plot_ct1(persons_fixed, title=f'(05) CT1: SynPop{YEAR}+ (before consistency rules)')

CT2

In [53]:
ax, ct2_fixed = plot_ct2(persons_fixed, title=f'(05) CT2: SynPop{YEAR}+ (before consistency rules)')

CT3

In [54]:
ax, ct3_fixed = plot_ct3(persons_fixed, title=f'(05) CT3: SynPop{YEAR}+ (before consistency rules)')

Forcing some Cross-Relationships Consistency Rules¶

For some age the control totals did not match perfectly so the cross relationships could not nicely be reproduced. Here I will force the structures that are important to us and see how much change is needed.

  • It is important that the total number of apprentice, students and pupils match
In [55]:
KEY_COLUMNS = ['person_id', 'age', 'current_edu', 'current_job_rank', 'is_employed']
In [56]:
persons_forced = persons_fixed[KEY_COLUMNS].copy(deep=True)

Forcing CT2 & CT3¶

In [57]:
_ = plot_ct2(persons_forced, title=f'(05) CT2: SynPop{YEAR}+')
In [58]:
_ = plot_ct3(persons_forced, title=f'(05) CT3: SynPop{YEAR}+')
In [59]:
# Forcing CT2
mask = persons_forced['current_edu'] == 'apprentice'
persons_forced['current_job_rank'] = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].mask(mask, 'apprentice')

mask = (persons_forced['current_edu'] != 'apprentice') & (persons_forced['current_job_rank'] == 'apprentice')
persons_forced['current_job_rank'] = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].mask(mask, 'null')
In [60]:
# Forcing CT3
mask = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].isin(['apprentice', 'employee', 'management'])
persons_forced['is_employed'] = persons_forced['is_employed'].mask(mask, True)

mask = persons_forced['current_job_rank'] == 'null'
persons_forced['is_employed'] = persons_forced['is_employed'].mask(mask, False)
In [61]:
_ = plot_ct2(persons_forced, title=f'(05) CT2: SynPop{YEAR}+ (forced)')
In [62]:
_ = plot_ct3(persons_forced, title=f'(05) CT3: SynPop{YEAR}+ (forced)')

Forcing CT1¶

In [63]:
ax, ct1_fixed = plot_ct1(persons_forced, title=f'(05a) CT1: SynPop{YEAR}+')
In [64]:
ist_scaled = ct1_ist.div(ct1_ist.sum(axis=1), axis=0).fillna(0).mul(ct1_fixed.sum(axis=1), axis=0)
ct1_diff = (ct1_fixed - ist_scaled).astype(int) 
print('Difference between fixed and forced:')
ct1_diff

Difference between fixed and forced:
Out[64]:
is_employed Employed Not Employed
kindergarten 0 0
pupil_primary 0 0
pupil_secondary 84351 -84351
apprentice 0 0
student 81170 -81170
null -653029 653029

Notes:

  • We have too many pupils and students working
  • We need to change some that are working to: "is_employed" = 0 and "current_job_rank" = "null"
  • There is no other way but to be tinkery at this point...

The changes that will be done:

  • All pupils_secondary which are "management" --> "null"
  • Reproduce 2017 CT1 structure by chaning the remaining pupils_secondary which are "employee" --> "null"
  • 10'000 students which are "management" --> "null"
  • Reproduce 2017 CT1 structure by chaning the remaining students which are "employee" --> "null"
  • Compensate by changing agents that have current_job_rant = "null" and current_edu="null" --> "employee"
In [65]:
def sample_non_active_persons(persons_df, persons_per_age):
    person_pool_mask = ((persons_df['current_edu'] == 'null') & 
                        (persons_df['current_job_rank'] == 'null') 
                        )
    weights = persons_forced['age'].map(persons_per_age.to_dict()).fillna(0) ** 2  #  why? These weights just work!
    person_ids_to_change = persons_df[person_pool_mask].sample(persons_per_age.sum(), weights=weights)['person_id'].values
    
    return person_ids_to_change

Fixing the pupil_secondary that should not be working

In [66]:
%%time
# All pupils_secondary - management --> null
mask = (persons_forced['current_edu'] == 'pupil_secondary') & (persons_forced['current_job_rank'] == 'management')
persons_forced['current_job_rank'] = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].mask(mask, 'null')
persons_forced['is_employed'] = persons_forced['is_employed'].mask(mask, False)

persons_changed = persons_forced[mask].groupby('age').count().iloc[:, 0].rename('persons_changed')

# Compensate for lost employment
person_ids_to_change = sample_non_active_persons(persons_forced, persons_changed)
sampled_mask = persons_forced['person_id'].isin(person_ids_to_change)

persons_forced['current_job_rank'] = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].mask(sampled_mask, 'employee')
persons_forced['is_employed'] = persons_forced['is_employed'].mask(sampled_mask, True)

Wall time: 5.13 s
In [67]:
%%time
# Fix rest with pupils_secondary - employee --> null
pupils_to_change = ct1_diff.loc['pupil_secondary', 'Employed'] - persons_changed.sum()
pupils_pool_mask = (persons_forced['current_edu'] == 'pupil_secondary') & (persons_forced['current_job_rank'] == 'employee')

pupils_to_change_mask = persons_forced['person_id'].isin(persons_forced[pupils_pool_mask].sample(pupils_to_change)['person_id'].values)

persons_forced['current_job_rank'] = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].mask(pupils_to_change_mask, 'null')
persons_forced['is_employed'] = persons_forced['is_employed'].mask(pupils_to_change_mask, False)

persons_changed = persons_forced[pupils_to_change_mask].groupby('age').count().iloc[:, 0].rename('persons_changed')

# Compensate for lost employment
person_ids_to_change = sample_non_active_persons(persons_forced, persons_changed)
sampled_mask = persons_forced['person_id'].isin(person_ids_to_change)

persons_forced['current_job_rank'] = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].mask(sampled_mask, 'employee')
persons_forced['is_employed'] = persons_forced['is_employed'].mask(sampled_mask, True)

Wall time: 5.41 s

Fixing the students that should not be working

In [68]:
%%time
# 10'000 students - management --> null
students_to_change = 10_000  # Based on what CT2 should look like 
student_pool_mask = (persons_forced['current_edu'] == 'student') & (persons_forced['current_job_rank'] == 'management')

students_to_change_mask = persons_forced['person_id'].isin(persons_forced[student_pool_mask].sample(students_to_change)['person_id'].values)

persons_forced['current_job_rank'] = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].mask(students_to_change_mask, 'null')
persons_forced['is_employed'] = persons_forced['is_employed'].mask(students_to_change_mask, False)

persons_changed = persons_forced[students_to_change_mask].groupby('age').count().iloc[:, 0].rename('persons_changed')

# Compensate for lost employment
person_ids_to_change = sample_non_active_persons(persons_forced, persons_changed)
sampled_mask = persons_forced['person_id'].isin(person_ids_to_change)

persons_forced['current_job_rank'] = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].mask(sampled_mask, 'employee')
persons_forced['is_employed'] = persons_forced['is_employed'].mask(sampled_mask, True)

Wall time: 5.15 s
In [69]:
%%time
# Fix rest with students - employee --> null
students_to_change = ct1_diff.loc['student', 'Employed'] - persons_changed.sum()
student_pool_mask = (persons_forced['current_edu'] == 'student') & (persons_forced['current_job_rank'] == 'employee')

students_to_change_mask = persons_forced['person_id'].isin(persons_forced[student_pool_mask].sample(students_to_change)['person_id'].values)

persons_forced['current_job_rank'] = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].mask(students_to_change_mask, 'null')
persons_forced['is_employed'] = persons_forced['is_employed'].mask(students_to_change_mask, False)

persons_changed = persons_forced[students_to_change_mask].groupby('age').count().iloc[:, 0].rename('persons_changed')

# Compensate for lost employment
person_ids_to_change = sample_non_active_persons(persons_forced, persons_changed)
sampled_mask = persons_forced['person_id'].isin(person_ids_to_change)

persons_forced['current_job_rank'] = persons_forced['current_job_rank'].mask(sampled_mask, 'employee')
persons_forced['is_employed'] = persons_forced['is_employed'].mask(sampled_mask, True)

Wall time: 5.66 s
In [70]:
ax, ct1_forced = plot_ct1(persons_forced, title=f'(05a) CT1: SynPop{YEAR}+')

Examining SynPop+ after applying consistency rules¶

Marginals Per Age¶

Is Employed

In [71]:
%%time
marginals_forced = marginal_fitting.compute_bfs_vs_scenario_marginal_summary_table(persons_forced, 
                                                                                   bfs_predicted_ratio, 
                                                                                   YEAR, 
                                                                                   feature='is_employed', 
                                                                                   control_level_list=['age']
                                                                                  )

counts_per_cat_forced = (marginals_forced
                         .reset_index()
                         .astype({'is_employed': str})  # to avoid boolean values as column names
                         .pivot(index='age', columns='is_employed', values=f'counts_{YEAR}')
                         .rename(columns={'True': 'true', 'False': 'false'})  # to avoid confusion between bool and str
                         )
counts_per_cat_forced = counts_per_cat_forced[['true', 'false']]

expected_true_counts_forced =  (marginals_forced[f'expected_counts_{YEAR}']
                               .reset_index()
                               .query('is_employed')
                               .astype({'is_employed': str})  # to avoid boolean values as column names
                               .groupby('age').sum().iloc[:, 0]
                               .rename('bfs: is_employed prediction')
                               )
expected_true_counts_forced.index = list(expected_true_counts_forced.index)  # otherwise, categorical

Wall time: 3.67 s
In [72]:
title = f'Employed People by Age - SynPop{YEAR}+ (FINAL)'

ax = plot_binary_feature_per_age_with_marginals(counts_per_cat_forced,
                                                expected_true_counts, 
                                                colour_dict=COLOUR_DICT, 
                                                ymax=150_000, 
                                                y_grid=True, 
                                                title=title
                                               )
    
save_figure(SAVE_OUTPUTS, f'05a_SynPop{YEAR}Plus_is_employed_by_age_FINAL.{FIG_FORMAT}',
            OUTPUT_DIR, FIG_DPI, FIG_FORMAT, FIG_BBOX_INCHES, FIG_PAD_INCHES)

2020-04-22 17:13:23,201 - INFO - root: Figure saved to file : ./outputs\05a_SynPop2040Plus_is_employed_by_age_FINAL.png

Current Education

In [73]:
marginals_edu = marginal_fitting.compute_ist_vs_scenario_marginal_counts(persons_forced, persons_ist,
                                                                         YEAR, YEAR_IST, 
                                                                         feature='current_edu', 
                                                                         control_level_list=['age']
                                                                         )

counts_per_cat_edu = (marginals_edu
                      .reset_index()
                      .pivot(index='age', columns='current_edu', values='counts_{}'.format(YEAR))
                      )

counts_per_cat_edu = counts_per_cat_edu[persons['current_edu'].cat.categories]
In [74]:
title = f'(05) Current Education by Age - SynPop{YEAR}+ (FINAL)'
edu_color_dict = {'kindergarten': 'k', 'pupil_primary': 'C0', 'pupil_secondary': 'C1', 'student': 'C2',
                  'apprentice': 'C3',  'null': 'grey'}

ax = plot_multi_class_feature_per_age(counts_per_cat_edu, colour_dict=edu_color_dict, ymax=150_000, title=title)

save_figure(SAVE_OUTPUTS, f'05a_SynPop{YEAR}Plus_current_edu_by_age_FINAL.{FIG_FORMAT}',
            OUTPUT_DIR, FIG_DPI, FIG_FORMAT, FIG_BBOX_INCHES, FIG_PAD_INCHES)

    persons_forced.set_index('person_id')['is_employed'].to_pickle(results_file, compression='gzip')
